close_reason: Positive decision. The sign_sync bridge pattern is correct and worth defending.
description: The pattern in sign_sync — detect whether a tokio runtime is active via Handle::try_current(), and if so spawn a scoped OS thread with its own current-thread runtime rather than calling block_on on the existing runtime — is exactly right. It avoids the nested block_on panic that bites every other naive sync-over-async bridge. The doc comment explains the tradeoff (thread overhead vs KMS latency). Resist any suggestion to 'simplify' this to a bare block_on or to require callers to be async.
title: sign_sync async bridge: correct and worth defending
